public_title: Efficacy of curcumin nano-formulation adjunct to surgery in stage I/II oral cavity cancer: a randomised study
scientific_title: Randomised controlled trial of nano-curcumin supplementation (2 g/day for 3 months post-surgery) versus placebo in patients with early stage oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma undergoing curative resection
health_condition: Early stage oral cavity squamous cell carcinoma

intervention.name: Nano-curcumin 2 g/day orally for 3 months post-surgery
intervention.details: Bioavailability-enhanced nano-curcumin capsules; two 500 mg capsules twice daily; started within 4 weeks of curative surgical resection

inclusion_criteria: Histologically confirmed squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity (lip, tongue, floor of mouth, buccal mucosa, gingiva, hard palate); Stage I (T1N0M0) or Stage II (T2N0M0) per AJCC 8th edition; Underwent curative surgical resection with clear margins (R0); Age 18 to 65 years; No post-operative radiotherapy planned; ECOG performance status 0 or 1; Adequate hepatic function (ALT/AST ≤2× ULN); Consent obtained
exclusion_criteria: Stage III or IV disease; Positive surgical margins (R1/R2 resection); Prior head and neck radiotherapy; Active systemic infection at time of enrolment; Pregnancy or lactation; Bile duct obstruction or cholelithiasis; Concurrent anti-coagulation therapy (warfarin, rivaroxaban); Concurrent participation in another interventional trial
primary_sponsor: Department of Science and Technology (DST), India
